L1.head: machine learning
L1.claim: Machine learning enables the optimization of refractive index profiles by analyzing THz imaging datasets

L1.head: refractive index profiles optimization
L1.claim: Optimized refractive index profiles enhance super-resolution capabilities

L2.head: bio-inspired design
L2.claim: Bio-inspired design reduces chromatic aberration by mimicking natural optical systems

L2.head: chromatic aberration reduction
L2.claim: Reduced chromatic aberration improves imaging performance in THz systems

L2.head: multi-material deposition
L2.claim: Multi-material deposition creates optical property gradients in metalenses

L2.head: optical property gradients
L2.claim: Optical property gradients enhance phase control capabilities

L2.head: phase control enhancement
L2.claim: Improved phase control increases numerical aperture and resolution

L3.head: adaptive optics
L3.claim: Adaptive optics integration enables real-time refractive index adjustment

L3.head: real-time refractive index adjustment
L3.claim: Dynamic refractive index adjustment optimizes phase distribution during imaging

L3.head: phase distribution optimization
L3.claim: Optimized phase distribution enhances imaging fidelity and resolution

L3.head: programmable metasurfaces
L3.claim: Combining programmable metasurfaces with nanopillars enables tunable focusing properties

L3.head: tunable focusing properties
L3.claim: Tunable focusing properties improve adaptability to specific imaging tasks

L3.head: adaptability improvement
L3.claim: Enhanced adaptability results in superior resolution across imaging scenarios
